etymology.description: 粉(粉) (fěn, "powder; pink") and 紅(紅) (hóng, "red") together form 粉紅(粉紅) (fěnhóng, "pink"), indicating the light shade of red. 玫瑰(玫瑰) (méiguī, "rose") refers to the flower.
etymology.interpretation: The term 粉紅(粉紅) (fěnhóng, "pink") combines 粉(粉) (fěn, "powder") to imply a softer, lighter version of 紅(紅) (hóng, "red"), creating the pastel color pink. 玫瑰(玫瑰) (méiguī, "rose") refers to the flower known for its association with romance and beauty. Together, 粉紅玫瑰(粉紅玫瑰) (fěnhóng méiguī, "pink rose") evokes the image of a rose colored in a gentle shade of pink.

examples.chinese: 她收到了很多粉红玫瑰(她收到了很多粉紅玫瑰)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shōu dào le hěn duō fěn hóng méi guī.

examples.chinese: 粉红玫瑰的香味(粉紅玫瑰的香味)令人陶醉(令人陶醉)。
examples.pinyin: Fěn hóng méi guī de xiāngwèi lìng rén táozuì.
examples.english: The fragrance of pink roses is intoxicating.

examples.chinese: 在花园里(在花園裡)，粉红玫瑰盛开的时候(粉紅玫瑰盛開的時候)，令人感到无比幸福(令人感到無比幸福)。
examples.pinyin: Zài huāyuán lǐ, fěn hóng méi guī shèngkāi de shíhòu, lìng rén gǎndào wúbǐ xìngfú.
examples.english: In the garden, when the pink roses are in full bloom, it brings incomparable happiness.
